learning_outcomes:
Applicable to students admitted in 2012-13 1.  |  Major Programme:
Knowledge and comprehension
Students will have acquired the following knowledge:
(1)  |  mathematical analysis,
(2)  |  linear algebra,
(3)  |  computer programming for mathematics,
(4)  |  further knowledge in at least one direction:
-  |  (Enrichment Stream) Knowledge in mathematics enabling the students
to have solid foundation for further
studies at the graduate school level; or
-  |  (Other streams) Knowledge in a specific field together with the
mathematics applicable to that field, for example, Computational and Applied
Mathematics Stream, Computational Big Data Analytics Stream.
(5)  |  mathematical literacy sufficient for various professions, for
example, research, education, financial technology, information technology,
data science, artificial intelligence, etc.
Skills
Generic Competencies
Students will have acquired a set of generic competencies.
(1)  |  ability to understand broad mathematical literature at the
university level independently;
(2)  |  awareness of mathematical problems arising in generic situations;
(3)  |  ability to formulate such problems mathematically, to find ways to
solve them;
(4)  |  ability to communicate progress of mathematical problem solving
effectively;
(5)  |  computer literacy and knowledge to use mathematical software in
solving problems.
Sectors of Professional Skills
In addition, students should possess professional skills in at least
one sector.
(1)  |  Graduate school research
(2)  |  Teaching
(3)  |  Mathematics intensive professions
(4)  |  Financial sector
(5)  |  Science and technology sector
Values and Attitudes
Upon graduation, our students will be able to apply their mathematical
knowledge generically in their future profession in a way that is unique for
mathematically trained experts. They should be able to
(1)  |  appreciate and advocate ways of thinking with logical rigour,
(2)  |  appreciate and take advantage of quantitative and analytical
exactitude,
(3)  |  appreciate the contribution of mathematics to the improvement and
formation of modern society built upon quantitative natural sciences.
2.  |  Minor Programme:
The minor programme of study aims at providing students with good
mathematical knowledge to support their professional development in today’s
knowledge society. In brief, upon completion of the programme, we anticipate
our students to have acquired the capability of formulating and modeling
problems in a logical sound and quantitatively efficient way, which is
typical in mathematical sciences.
Knowledge and comprehension
Students will have acquired the following knowledge:
(1)  |  Calculus or linear algebra,
(2)  |  mathematical literacy sufficient for supporting a particular
profession, including but not limited to, research, education, financial
technology, information technology, data science, artificial intelligence,
etc.
(3)  |  model formulation related to a particular profession.
Skills
Students will have acquired a set of generic competencies listed
below.
(1)  |  ability to understand broad mathematical literature related to
their own professional development at the university level independently;
(2)  |  awareness of mathematical problems arising in generic situations;
(3)  |  ability to formulate such problems mathematically, to find ways to
model them;
(4)  |  ability to communicate progress of mathematical modelling
effectively.
Values and Attitudes
Upon completion of the programme, our students are expected to be
able to apply their mathematical knowledge generically in their future
profession in a way that is unique for mathematically trained experts, they
should be able to
(1)  |  appreciate, advocate, and take advantage of ways of quantitative
and analytical thinking,
(2)  |  appreciate the contribution of mathematics to the improvement and
formation of modern society built upon quantitative natural sciences.
study_scheme:
Mathematics Mathematics Applicable
to students admitted in 2025-26
Students
admitted to the Mathematics Programme through the broad-based admission
scheme are automatically placed in Mathematics Stream. A student who
plans to graduate in any other stream(s) may select the relevant courses
as prescribed below, and apply at the final year of attendance to
specialize in the stream(s) concerned. To specialize in the Enrichment
Stream at their final year of attendance, such a student must also have
obtained a Cumulative Major GPA of 3.3 or above, or the preceding two
semesters’ Major GPA of 3.3 or above. Students admitted to the Enrichment
Mathematics Programme are automatically placed in the Enrichment Stream; so no declaration is required.
Major Programme Requirement
Students are required to
complete a minimum of 71 units of courses as follows:
Units
1.  |  Faculty Package:  |  9
Group C: MATH1010/1018 or 1030/1038 Group E: STAT1011 A course from the following Group A: LSCI1001 or 1002 Group B: CHEM1070 or 1072 or 1280 Group D: PHYS1001 or 1002 or 1111 or 1113
For students who
specialize in streams other than Enrichment Stream
2.  |  Required Courses:  |  29
(a)  |  MATH1010/1018 or 1030/1038, 1025/1028, 1090/1098, 2010/2018,
2020/2028, 2040/2048, 2050/2058, 2060/2068, 2221
(b)  |  Capstone
course: Either MATH4400 or 4900
3.  |  Elective Courses:  |  33
(a)  |  9 units from MATH2070/2078, 2230, 3270, 3280 (or
STAT2001#); and
(b)  |  24 units from at most one stream below [a][b]:
For students who specialize in Computational
and Applied Mathematics Stream:
(i)  |  CSCI1540# and MATH3230; and
(ii)  |  9-12 units from MATH32xx, 33xx, 42xx, 43xx; and 0-3
units from IERG5124#, SEEM3550#/ESTR3506#, SEEM3570#/ESTR3508#; and
(iii)  |  6 units of MATH courses at 3000 or above level
For students who specialize in Computational
Big Data Analytics Stream:
(i)  |  9-12 units from MATH3320, 3330, 3340, 4280; and
(ii)  |  3-9 units from MATH3215, 3230, 3280, 3290, 3310,
3360, 4230, 4240; and 0-3 units from IERG5124#, SEEM3550#/ESTR3506#,
STAT4005#; and
(iii)  |  6 units of MATH courses at 3000 or above level
For students in the Mathematics Stream:
(i)  |  18-24 units of MATH courses at 3000 or above level; and
(ii)  |  0-6 units of either BMED courses at 3000 or above
level, or courses at 2000 or above level of one non-MATH subject area[c] offered
by the Faculties/Departments of Science, Engineering, Economics and
Finance. The specific subject area codes are given in explanatory note[d].
For students who specialize in Mathematics-Education
Stream[a]:
(i)  |  9-18 units of BMED courses at 3000 or above level; and
(ii)  |  6-15 units of MATH courses at 3000 or above level
For students who specialize in Mathematics-Multidisciplinary
Stream:
(i)  |  9-18 units of courses at 2000 or above level of at
most two non-MATH subject areas[c] offered by the Faculties/Departments
of Science, Engineering, Economics, and Finance. The specific subject
area codes are given in explanatory note[d]; and
(ii)  |  6-15 units of MATH courses at 3000 or above level
Total:  |  71
For
students who specialize in Enrichment
Stream[b]:
2.  |  Required Courses:  |  35
(a)  |  MATH1010/1018 or 1030/1038,
1025/1028, 1090/1098, 2010/2018, 2020/2028, 2040/2048, 2050/2058,
2060/2068, 2070/2078, 2221, 2230
(b)  |  Capstone course: Either
MATH4400 or 4900
3.  |  Elective Courses:  |  27
(a)  |  15-21 units from MATH3030, 3040,
3060, 3070, 3093, 3230, 3340, 4010, 4030, 4050, 4060, 4080, 4220, 5011,
5012, 5021, 5022, 5031, 5032, 5051, 5052, 5061, 5062, 5070; and
(b)  |  0-3 units
from MATH3240, 4280; and
(c)  |  0-3 units
from MATH3320, 4230; and
(d)  |  0-6 units
from MATH3270, 3280; and
(e)  |  0-6 units
of MATH courses at 3000 or above level
Total:  |  71
In addition to fulfilling the above Major Programme
Requirement, students meeting the criteria as specified by the Faculty
can take the following stream offered by the Faculty:
Science, Technology And
Research Stream
Students are required to complete
a minimum of 12 units of courses as follows:
Units
1.  |  Required
Courses:
(a)  |  One Faculty Package Course:  |  3
Choose from the two remaining groups of the Faculty
Package that have not been used to fulfill the Faculty Package Requirement
(b)  |  Research Courses:  |  6
STAR2000, 3000, 4000[e]
(c)  |  Seminar Courses:  |  3
STAR2050, 3050, 4050
2.  |  Experiential Learning:  |  ---
At least 4 consecutive weeks of outside Hong Kong
exposure[f]
Total:  |  12
Explanatory Notes:
1.  |  MATH1010, 1018, 1025, 1028, 1030, 1038, 1090, 1098, MATH
courses at 2000 and above level, BMED courses at 3000 and above level,
courses at 2000 and above level of the subject areas listed in [d] below
as well as those labeled as # will be included in the calculation of
Major GPA for honours classification if students specialize in the
respective streams.
[a]  |  Students specialized in Mathematics-Education Stream may declare at most one extra stream from (1) Computational and Applied
Mathematics Stream, (2) Computational Big Data Analytics Stream, (3)
Mathematics-Multidisciplinary Stream or (4) Science, Technology And
Research Stream. Students
who have satisfied the requirements of both streams will be allowed to graduate with
double streams.
[b]  |  Students specialized in Enrichment
Stream may declare at most one extra stream from (1)
Computational and Applied Mathematics Stream, (2) Computational Big Data
Analytics Stream, (3) Mathematics-Education Stream, (4)
Mathematics-Multidisciplinary Stream or (5) Science, Technology And
Research Stream. Students
who have satisfied the requirements of both streams will be allowed to graduate with
double streams.
[c]  |  Students shall declare the subject area(s) at least
one term before graduation.
[d]  |  Faculty/ Department  |  Subject Area Code
Science  |  BCHE, BIOL, CHEM, CMBI, EESC, ENSC, ESSC, FNSC, LSCI,
MBTE, PHYS, RMSC, STAT
Engineering  |  AIST, BMEG, CENG, CSCI, EEEN, ELEG, ENGG, ESTR, FTEC,
IERG, MAEG, SEEM
Economics/Finance  |  DOTE, ECON, FINA, GLEF
[e]  |  Students may select research-oriented course(s), as
approved by the Major Programme, to substitute up to 4 units for
fulfillment of Research Courses requirement.
[f]  |  Students must complete any
exchange/research/internship programme(s) offered by the University,
Colleges, the Faculty of Science or Major Programme, as approved by the
Major Programme, to fulfill the Experiential Learning requirement.
Students are responsible for the extra costs incurred in the exchange/research/internship
programme(s).

@  |  For students who intend to graduate in the single stream
specialization in Enrichment Stream, it is better to take MATH2058, 2068,
2078, 2230 as early as possible (most preferably during the Second Year of
Attendance). For students who plan to graduate in one of the double streams
specified in Explanatory Note [b], a postponement of some of MATH2058, 2068,
2078, 2230 to no later than the Third Year of Attendance may be considered,
so as to facilitate the fulfillment of the requirements for the add-on
stream. Nevertheless, students should take into account, in the planning of
courses to be taken in fulfillment of Elective Courses 3(a) for the
Enrichment Stream, that one or several of MATH2058, 2068, 2078, 2230 may be
the pre-requisites of various courses listed in Elective Courses 3(a). A
delay in taking any one of MATH2058, 2068, 2078, 2230 may result in being
barred from taking some course(s) listed in Elective Courses 3(a).

@  |  Students may take MATH4400 as a
substitute for STAR4000.
+  |  Students who plan to graduate in any
one stream, or any one of the double streams specified in Explanatory Note [a]
and [b] may replace MATH1010, 1025, 1030, 1090, 2010, 2020, 2040, 2050, 2060,
2070 with MATH1018, 1028, 1038, 1098, 2018, 2028, 2048, 2058, 2068, 2078
respectively.
∞  |  Students who plan to graduate in the
Enrichment Stream or any one of the double streams specified in Explanatory
Note [b] may replace MATH1018, 1028, 1038, 1098, 2018, 2028, 2048, 2058,
2068, 2078 with MATH1010, 1025, 1030, 1090, 2010, 2020, 2040, 2050, 2060,
2070 respectively.
Minor Programme Title Mathematics
Minor Programme Requirement
Students are required to complete a minimum of 18
units of courses, with at least 6 units at 3000 or above
level, as follows:
Units
1.  |  Elective Courses:  |  18
18 units of MATH courses, out of which at least 6
units of courses are at 3000 to 5000 level
Total:  |  18
Explanatory Notes:
1.  |  This Minor Programme is not applicable to students
who major in Mathematics, Mathematics and Information Engineering,
Mathematics and Mathematics Education and Interdisciplinary Data
Analytics & X Double Major Programme with second Major in Mathematics
or Mathematics and Applied Mathematics.
2.  |  No more than 9 units of courses
taken to fulfill the requirements of the students’ Major Programme(s) and
other Minor Programme(s) respectively can be used to fulfill the
requirement of this Minor Programme.
